rulings.Case Title: ERNEST MOUTON (PROVIDE ACCESS) VS. GOODRICH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 20, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Nancy T. Williams, Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel: hearing required. The hearing will be at 1:30p.m. in Department 206, concurrent with the Trial Setting Conference. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE ALRIDGE VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 800, INCLUSIVE, AS REQUIRED BY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 20, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant The Pep Boys - Manny, Moe & Jack of California's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Defendant did not submit evidence demonstrating that plaintiff's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to the increased risk of plaintiff developing his asbestos-related disease. Defendant's separate statement does not include any facts supporting such assertion. Even if the Court could and did use its discretion to consider the arguments defendant makes related to the deposition testimony of Dr. Hammar and Dr. Brody, such transcripts are inadmissible. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibits J and K are sustained. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. It appears defendant did not attempt to sustain its burden via this prong. However if it did, defendant's separate statement does not include facts to sufficiently demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claim that exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
